title: King Charles Laughs Off Baby's Less-Than-Thrilled Reaction to Meeting Him

sections.heading: Visit to Lossiemouth
sections.content: King Charles III visited RAF Lossiemouth in Moray on 6 August 2025 for the stand-up parade of No. 42 (Torpedo Bomber) Squadron. Public reports said he watched a flypast by two Typhoon jets and a P-8A Poseidon before re-presenting the squadron's Standard and meeting senior RAF personnel.

sections.heading: Base and squadron context
sections.content: Open coverage of the visit described RAF Lossiemouth as Scotland's only RAF main operating base and one of the two stations providing Quick Reaction Alert for UK airspace. Forces News said No. 42 Squadron was re-formed in 2023 as the combined operational conversion unit for RAF crews and engineers on the P-8A Poseidon fleet and the future E-7 Wedgetail force. The King also toured the Poseidon hangar and one of the base's maritime patrol aircraft.

sections.heading: Walkabout moment
sections.content: The lighter moment highlighted by People came as Charles met families and children from the Airplay youth support programme and the wider station community. Public summaries of the footage said one baby burst into tears as the King approached, prompting a joke from Charles before he carried on speaking with the family.
